issue_text: Use of weak MD5 hash for security. Consider usedforsecurity=False
label: FP
reasoning: azericard.py:41 (bank_data) — `nonce = substr(hashlib.md5(str(irand).encode()).hexdigest(), 0, 16)`. MD5 here is being used as a hex formatter to produce 16 hex characters from an integer; no security property of MD5 (collision/preimage resistance) is relied upon. Authentication of the request is done by `hash_hmac('sha1', to_sign, key_for_sign)` on line 52. The MD5 call leaks no key material and creates no attack surface — it is purely a format transform. This is a False Positive on the MD5 weak-hash detector (Bandit B303), not a NON_ACTIONABLE protocol mandate. The AzeriCard MPI spec mandates a 16-hex NONCE field, not MD5 specifically; per the project CLAUDE.md guidance (sec. 3d 'stronger wrapper' / non-security use), MD5 used purely for byte-shaping is FP.
